array_types.name: INTERP0_CTRL_LANE0_OVERF_Field_Array
array_types.code:
type INTERP0_CTRL_LANE0_OVERF_Field_Array is array (0 .. 2) of Boolean
  with Component_Size => 1, Size => 3;

array_types.name: INTERP1_CTRL_LANE0_OVERF_Field_Array
array_types.code:
type INTERP1_CTRL_LANE0_OVERF_Field_Array is array (0 .. 2) of Boolean
  with Component_Size => 1, Size => 3;

record_types.name: DIV_CSR_Register

fields.name: READY
fields.description:
Read-only. Changes to 1 when any register is written, and back to 0
when QUOTIENT is read.\n Software can use this flag to make
save/restore more efficient (skip if not DIRTY).\n If the flag is
used in this way, it's recommended to either read QUOTIENT only,\n or
REMAINDER and then QUOTIENT, to prevent data loss on context switch.

fields.name: DIRTY
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: FIFO_ST_Register

fields.name: VLD
fields.description:
Read-only. Value is 1 if this core's TX FIFO is not full (i.e. if
FIFO_WR is ready for more data)

fields.name: RDY
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Sticky flag indicating the TX FIFO was written when
full. This write was ignored by the FIFO.

fields.name: WOF
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Sticky flag indicating the RX FIFO was read when empty.
This read was ignored by the FIFO.

fields.name: ROE
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: INTERP0_CTRL_LANE0_OVERF_Field
INTERP0_CTRL_LANE0_Register

fields.name: SHIFT
fields.description: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

fields.name: MASK_LSB
fields.description:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)\n
Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

fields.name: MASK_MSB
fields.description:
If SIGNED is set, the shifted and masked accumulator value is
sign-extended to 32 bits\n before adding to BASE0, and LANE0 PEEK/POP
appear extended to 32 bits when read by processor.

fields.name: SIGNED
fields.description:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's accumulator into this lane's shift +
mask hardware.\n Takes effect even if ADD_RAW is set (the CROSS_INPUT
mux is before the shift+mask bypass)

fields.name: CROSS_INPUT
fields.description:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's result into this lane's accumulator on
POP.

fields.name: CROSS_RESULT
fields.description:
If 1, mask + shift is bypassed for LANE0 result. This does not affect
FULL result.

fields.name: ADD_RAW
fields.description:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on
the bus.\n No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using
a lane to generate sequence\n of pointers into flash or SRAM.

fields.name: FORCE_MSB
fields.description:
Only present on INTERP0 on each core. If BLEND mode is enabled:\n -
LANE1 result is a linear interpolation between BASE0 and BASE1,
controlled\n by the 8 LSBs of lane 1 shift and mask value (a
fractional number between\n 0 and 255/256ths)\n - LANE0 result does
not have BASE0 added (yields only the 8 LSBs of lane 1 shift+mask
value)\n - FULL result does not have lane 1 shift+mask value added
(BASE2 + lane 0 shift+mask)\n LANE1 SIGNED flag controls whether the
interpolation is signed or unsigned.

fields.name: BLEND
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_22_22
fields.description: Read-only. Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM0 are set.

fields.name: OVERF
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: FORCE_MSB

INTERP1_CTRL_LANE0_Register

fields.name: SHIFT
fields.description: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

fields.name: MASK_LSB
fields.description:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)\n
Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

fields.name: MASK_MSB
fields.description:
If SIGNED is set, the shifted and masked accumulator value is
sign-extended to 32 bits\n before adding to BASE0, and LANE0 PEEK/POP
appear extended to 32 bits when read by processor.

fields.name: SIGNED
fields.description:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's accumulator into this lane's shift +
mask hardware.\n Takes effect even if ADD_RAW is set (the CROSS_INPUT
mux is before the shift+mask bypass)

fields.name: CROSS_INPUT
fields.description:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's result into this lane's accumulator on
POP.

fields.name: CROSS_RESULT
fields.description:
If 1, mask + shift is bypassed for LANE0 result. This does not affect

fields.name: ADD_RAW
fields.description:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on
the bus.\n No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using
a lane to generate sequence\n of pointers into flash or SRAM.

fields.name: FORCE_MSB
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_21_21
fields.description:
Only present on INTERP1 on each core. If CLAMP mode is enabled:\n -
LANE0 result is shifted and masked ACCUM0, clamped by a lower bound
of\n BASE0 and an upper bound of BASE1.\n - Signedness of these
comparisons is determined by LANE0_CTRL_SIGNED

fields.name: CLAMP
fields.description: Read-only. Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM0 are set.

fields.name: OVERF
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: SIO_Peripheral

fields.name: CPUID
fields.description: Input value for GPIO pins

fields.name: GPIO_IN
fields.description: Input value for QSPI pins

fields.name: GPIO_HI_IN
fields.description: GPIO output value

fields.name: GPIO_OUT
fields.description: GPIO output value set

fields.name: GPIO_OUT_SET
fields.description: GPIO output value clear

fields.name: GPIO_OUT_CLR
fields.description: GPIO output value XOR

fields.name: GPIO_OUT_XOR
fields.description: GPIO output enable

fields.name: GPIO_OE
fields.description: GPIO output enable set

fields.name: GPIO_OE_SET
fields.description: GPIO output enable clear

fields.name: GPIO_OE_CLR
fields.description: GPIO output enable XOR

fields.name: GPIO_OE_XOR
fields.description: QSPI output value

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OUT
fields.description: QSPI output value set

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OUT_SET
fields.description: QSPI output value clear

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OUT_CLR
fields.description: QSPI output value XOR

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OUT_XOR
fields.description: QSPI output enable

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OE
fields.description: QSPI output enable set

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OE_SET
fields.description: QSPI output enable clear

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OE_CLR
fields.description: QSPI output enable XOR

fields.name: GPIO_HI_OE_XOR
fields.description:
Status register for inter-core FIFOs (mailboxes).\n There is one FIFO
in the core 0 -> core 1 direction, and one core 1 -> core 0. Both are
32 bits wide and 8 words deep.\n Core 0 can see the read side of the
1->0 FIFO (RX), and the write side of 0->1 FIFO (TX).\n Core 1 can
see the read side of the 0->1 FIFO (RX), and the write side of 1->0
FIFO (TX).\n The SIO IRQ for each core is the logical OR of the VLD,
WOF and ROE fields of its FIFO_ST register.

fields.name: FIFO_ST
fields.description: Write access to this core's TX FIFO

fields.name: FIFO_WR
fields.description: Read access to this core's RX FIFO

fields.name: FIFO_RD
fields.description:
Spinlock state\n A bitmap containing the state of all 32 spinlocks
(1=locked).\n Mainly intended for debugging.

fields.name: SPINLOCK_ST
fields.description:
Divider unsigned dividend\n Write to the DIVIDEND operand of the
divider, i.e. the p in `p / q`.\n Any operand write starts a new
calculation. The results appear in QUOTIENT, REMAINDER.\n
UDIVIDEND/SDIVIDEND are aliases of the same internal register. The U
alias starts an\n unsigned calculation, and the S alias starts a
signed calculation.

fields.name: DIV_UDIVIDEND
fields.description:
Divider unsigned divisor\n Write to the DIVISOR operand of the
divider, i.e. the q in `p / q`.\n Any operand write starts a new
calculation. The results appear in QUOTIENT, REMAINDER.\n
UDIVIDEND/SDIVIDEND are aliases of the same internal register. The U
alias starts an\n unsigned calculation, and the S alias starts a
signed calculation.

fields.name: DIV_UDIVISOR
fields.description:
Divider signed dividend\n The same as UDIVIDEND, but starts a signed
calculation, rather than unsigned.

fields.name: DIV_SDIVIDEND
fields.description:
Divider signed divisor\n The same as UDIVISOR, but starts a signed
calculation, rather than unsigned.

fields.name: DIV_SDIVISOR
fields.description:
Divider result quotient\n The result of `DIVIDEND / DIVISOR`
(division). Contents undefined while CSR_READY is low.\n For signed
calculations, QUOTIENT is negative when the signs of DIVIDEND and
DIVISOR differ.\n This register can be written to directly, for
context save/restore purposes. This halts any\n in-progress
calculation and sets the CSR_READY and CSR_DIRTY flags.\n Reading
from QUOTIENT clears the CSR_DIRTY flag, so should read results in
the order\n REMAINDER, QUOTIENT if CSR_DIRTY is used.

fields.name: DIV_QUOTIENT
fields.description:
Divider result remainder\n The result of `DIVIDEND % DIVISOR`
(modulo). Contents undefined while CSR_READY is low.\n For signed
calculations, REMAINDER is negative only when DIVIDEND is negative.\n
This register can be written to directly, for context save/restore
purposes. This halts any\n in-progress calculation and sets the
CSR_READY and CSR_DIRTY flags.

fields.name: DIV_REMAINDER
fields.description: Control and status register for divider.

fields.name: DIV_CSR
fields.description: Read/write access to accumulator 0

fields.name: INTERP0_ACCUM0
fields.description: Read/write access to accumulator 1

fields.name: INTERP0_ACCUM1
fields.description: Read/write access to BASE0 register.

fields.name: INTERP0_BASE0
fields.description: Read/write access to BASE1 register.

fields.name: INTERP0_BASE1
fields.description: Read/write access to BASE2 register.

fields.name: INTERP0_BASE2
fields.description:
Read LANE0 result, and simultaneously write lane results to both
accumulators (POP).

fields.name: INTERP0_POP_LANE0
fields.description:
Read LANE1 result, and simultaneously write lane results to both
accumulators (POP).

fields.name: INTERP0_POP_LANE1
fields.description:
Read FULL result, and simultaneously write lane results to both
accumulators (POP).

fields.name: INTERP0_POP_FULL
fields.description: Read LANE0 result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

fields.name: INTERP0_PEEK_LANE0
fields.description: Read LANE1 result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

fields.name: INTERP0_PEEK_LANE1
fields.description: Read FULL result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

fields.name: INTERP0_PEEK_FULL
fields.description: Control register for lane 0

fields.name: INTERP0_CTRL_LANE0
fields.description: Control register for lane 1

fields.name: INTERP0_CTRL_LANE1
fields.description:
Values written here are atomically added to ACCUM0\n Reading yields
lane 0's raw shift and mask value (BASE0 not added).

fields.name: INTERP0_ACCUM0_ADD
fields.description:
Values written here are atomically added to ACCUM1\n Reading yields
lane 1's raw shift and mask value (BASE1 not added).

fields.name: INTERP0_ACCUM1_ADD
fields.description:
On write, the lower 16 bits go to BASE0, upper bits to BASE1
simultaneously.\n Each half is sign-extended to 32 bits if that
lane's SIGNED flag is set.

fields.name: INTERP0_BASE_1AND0
fields.description: Read/write access to accumulator 0

fields.name: INTERP1_ACCUM0
fields.description: Read/write access to accumulator 1

fields.name: INTERP1_ACCUM1
fields.description: Read/write access to BASE0 register.

fields.name: INTERP1_BASE0
fields.description: Read/write access to BASE1 register.

fields.name: INTERP1_BASE1
fields.description: Read/write access to BASE2 register.

fields.name: INTERP1_BASE2
fields.description:
Read LANE0 result, and simultaneously write lane results to both
accumulators (POP).

fields.name: INTERP1_POP_LANE0
fields.description:
Read LANE1 result, and simultaneously write lane results to both
accumulators (POP).

fields.name: INTERP1_POP_LANE1
fields.description:
Read FULL result, and simultaneously write lane results to both
accumulators (POP).

fields.name: INTERP1_POP_FULL
fields.description: Read LANE0 result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

fields.name: INTERP1_PEEK_LANE0
fields.description: Read LANE1 result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

fields.name: INTERP1_PEEK_LANE1
fields.description: Read FULL result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

fields.name: INTERP1_PEEK_FULL
fields.description: Control register for lane 0

fields.name: INTERP1_CTRL_LANE0
fields.description: Control register for lane 1

fields.name: INTERP1_CTRL_LANE1
fields.description:
Values written here are atomically added to ACCUM0\n Reading yields

fields.name: INTERP1_ACCUM0_ADD

fields.name: INTERP1_BASE_1AND0
fields.description:
Reading from a spinlock address will:
 - Return 0 if lock is already
locked
 - Otherwise return nonzero, and simultaneously claim the
lock

 Writing (any value) releases the lock.
 If core 0 and core
1 attempt to claim the same lock simultaneously, core 0 wins.
 The
value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

fields.name: SPINLOCK0
fields.description:
Reading from a spinlock address will:
 - Return 0 if lock is already
locked
 - Otherwise return nonzero, and simultaneously claim the
lock

 Writing (any value) releases the lock.
 If core 0 and core
1 attempt to claim the same lock simultaneously, core 0 wins.
 The
value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

 If core 0 and core
1 attempt to claim the same lock simultaneously, core 0 wins.
 The
value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

fields.name: SPINLOCK22
fields.description:
Reading from a spinlock address will:
 - Return 0 if lock is already
locked
 - Otherwise return nonzero, and simultaneously claim the
lock

 Writing (any value) releases the lock.
 If core 0 and core
1 attempt to claim the same lock simultaneously, core 0 wins.
 The
value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

variables.name: SIO_Periph
variables.code:
SIO_Periph : aliased SIO_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => SIO_Base;
